steps.title: Identify the punctuation task
steps.content: The sentence reads: "Nigerian expressionist ______ was known for his vibrant paintings." The blank must complete a noun phrase in which a name identifies which Nigerian expressionist painter is meant. A name used this way is a restrictive appositive and takes no surrounding commas.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice by punctuation rule
steps.content:
• Choice A — painter, Gani Odutokun,: two commas make the name non-restrictive, implying it is extra information rather than an identifying label — incorrect here. ✗
  • Choice B — painter Gani Odutokun.: a period mid-sentence creates a fragment; what follows the period has no independent subject. ✗
  • Choice C — painter Gani Odutokun: no commas or period — the name sits directly in the noun phrase and the sentence continues correctly. ✓
  • Choice D — painter, Gani Odutokun: a single comma after "painter" opens a non-restrictive phrase without closing it, creating an asymmetric and ungrammatical break. ✗
That matches Choice C — painter Gani Odutokun.
